principles.name: pacta-sunt-servanda
principles.description: Agreements must be kept - the foundational principle of contract law
principles.applicationContext: Binding force of contracts between parties

principles.name: consensus-ad-idem
principles.description: Meeting of the minds - parties must have mutual agreement

principles.name: exceptio-non-adimpleti-contractus
principles.description: Exception of non-performance - a party need not perform if the other has not

principles.name: consideration-exists
principles.description: Valid contracts require consideration or quid pro quo
principles.applicationContext: Contract formation in common law jurisdictions

principles.name: nemo-plus-iuris
principles.description: No one can transfer more rights than they have

principles.name: nemo-dat-quod-non-habet
principles.description: No one gives what they do not have

principles.name: res-nullius
principles.description: A thing belonging to no one - can be acquired by occupation

principles.name: audi-alteram-partem
principles.description: Hear the other side - fundamental principle of natural justice
principles.applicationContext: Fair hearing rights in all proceedings

principles.name: nemo-iudex-in-causa-sua
principles.description: No one should be a judge in their own cause

principles.name: ei-qui-affirmat-non-ei-qui-negat-incumbit-probatio
principles.description: The burden of proof lies on the one who affirms, not on the one who denies

principles.name: lex-specialis-derogat-legi-generali
principles.description: Specific law overrides general law

principles.name: lex-posterior-derogat-legi-priori
principles.description: Later law overrides earlier law

principles.name: expressio-unius-est-exclusio-alterius
principles.description: Express mention of one thing excludes others

principles.name: nullum-crimen-sine-lege
principles.description: No crime without law - acts cannot be criminal unless prohibited by law
principles.provenance: Enlightenment legal philosophy, international human rights
principles.applicationContext: Principle of legality in criminal law

principles.name: nulla-poena-sine-lege
principles.description: No punishment without law - punishment must be prescribed by law
principles.provenance: Enlightenment legal philosophy, international human rights

principles.name: in-dubio-pro-reo
principles.description: When in doubt, for the accused - presumption of innocence
principles.applicationContext: Criminal trials and burden of proof

principles.name: actus-non-facit-reum-nisi-mens-sit-rea
principles.description: An act does not make one guilty unless the mind is guilty - requires both actus reus and mens rea

principles.name: damnum-injuria-datum
principles.description: Loss wrongfully caused - basis of delictual liability

principles.name: volenti-non-fit-injuria
principles.description: No injury is done to one who consents
principles.applicationContext: Defense to liability based on consent

principles.name: culpa
principles.description: Fault or negligence - required element for liability

principles.name: res-ipsa-loquitur
principles.description: The thing speaks for itself - doctrine of negligence

principles.name: supremacy-of-constitution
principles.description: The constitution is supreme and all law must conform to it

principles.name: rule-of-law
principles.description: Everyone is subject to the law and equal before it

principles.name: separation-of-powers
principles.description: Division of government into legislative, executive, and judicial branches

principles.name: ubuntu
principles.description: Humanity towards others - African philosophy of interconnectedness
principles.provenance: African customary law, South African Constitution

principles.name: legality
principles.description: Administrative action must be authorized by law

principles.name: rationality
principles.description: Administrative decisions must be rational and reasonable

principles.name: procedural-fairness
principles.description: Fair procedures must be followed in administrative action

principles.name: legitimate-expectation
principles.description: Reasonable expectations created by authorities should be protected

principles.name: equity-will-not-suffer-a-wrong-without-remedy
principles.description: Equity provides remedies where common law fails

principles.name: he-who-seeks-equity-must-do-equity
principles.description: One seeking equitable relief must act fairly

principles.name: equality-is-equity
principles.description: Equity treats all parties equally

principles.name: equity-follows-the-law
principles.description: Equitable principles supplement but do not contradict law

principles.name: onus-probandi
principles.description: Burden of proof - party asserting must prove

principles.name: best-evidence-rule
principles.description: Original evidence is preferred over secondary evidence

principles.name: relevance
principles.description: Evidence must be relevant to the matter at issue

principles.name: hearsay-rule
principles.description: Hearsay evidence is generally inadmissible

principles.name: literal-rule
principles.description: Words in statutes should be given their ordinary meaning

principles.name: golden-rule
principles.description: Literal interpretation unless it leads to absurdity

principles.name: mischief-rule
principles.description: Interpret to suppress the mischief and advance the remedy

principles.name: purposive-approach
principles.description: Interpret statutes according to their purpose and spirit

principles.name: tempus-regit-actum
principles.description: Time governs the act - law in force at the time governs

principles.name: prescription
principles.description: Rights can be lost through passage of time and inaction

principles.name: laches
principles.description: Unreasonable delay can bar equitable relief
principles.applicationContext: Delay as bar to equitable remedies

principles.name: restitutio-in-integrum
principles.description: Restoration to the original position

principles.name: specific-performance
principles.description: Actual performance of contractual obligation may be ordered
principles.applicationContext: Equitable remedy for breach of contract

principles.name: injunction
principles.description: Court order to do or refrain from doing something

principles.name: ubi-ius-ibi-remedium
principles.description: Where there is a right, there is a remedy

principles.name: doli-incapax
principles.description: Incapable of crime - children under certain age presumed incapable

principles.name: compos-mentis
principles.description: Of sound mind - legal capacity requires mental competence

principles.name: bona-fides
principles.description: Good faith - acting honestly and fairly
principles.applicationContext: Standard of conduct in legal relations

principles.name: contra-bonos-mores
principles.description: Against good morals - unlawful conduct violating community values

principles.name: ex-turpi-causa-non-oritur-actio
principles.description: From a dishonourable cause, no action arises

principles.name: uberrima-fides
principles.description: Utmost good faith - highest standard of honesty
principles.applicationContext: Duty of disclosure in insurance contracts

principles.name: qui-facit-per-alium-facit-per-se
principles.description: He who acts through another acts himself - vicarious liability

principles.name: respondeat-superior
principles.description: Let the master answer - employer liability for employee acts

principles.name: causa-sine-qua-non
principles.description: Cause without which not - but-for test of causation

principles.name: novus-actus-interveniens
principles.description: New intervening act - breaks chain of causation

principles.name: proportionality
principles.description: Measures must be proportionate to their objectives
principles.applicationContext: Limitation of rights and administrative action

principles.name: subsidiarity
principles.description: Decisions should be made at the most local level possible
principles.applicationContext: Allocation of powers in multi-level governance

principles.name: non-refoulement
principles.description: Prohibition on returning refugees to danger

principles.name: jus-cogens
principles.description: Peremptory norms of international law from which no derogation is permitted
principles.provenance: Vienna Convention on the Law of Treaties

principles.name: pacta-tertiis-nec-nocent-nec-prosunt
principles.description: Treaties neither harm nor benefit third parties
principles.applicationContext: Effect of treaties on third states

rules.name: resolve-sabotage-claim
rules.description: Resolve sabotage claim with enhanced evidence mapping

rules.name: resolve-bad-faith-claim
rules.description:
Resolve bad faith claims with contextual analysis
   
   BAD FAITH ELEMENTS:
   1. Dishonest or improper motive
   2. Abuse of process or power
   3. Lack of genuine belief in claim
   4. Ulterior purpose
   
   CASE-SPECIFIC INDICATORS:
   - Director loan system hypocrisy (Peter's own withdrawals vs. characterization of Daniel's)
   - Timing of interdict (immediately after fraud discovery)
   - Manufactured urgency claims
   - Selective application of standards
   - Weaponization of trust powers
   
   LEGAL PRINCIPLES:
   - Bona fides requirement
   - Abuse of process
   - Estoppel
   - Clean hands doctrine

rules.name: resolve-fraud-claim
rules.description:
Resolve fraud claims with forensic analysis
   
   FRAUD ELEMENTS:
   1. Misrepresentation (false statement)
   2. Knowledge of falsity (scienter)
   3. Intention to deceive
   4. Reliance
   5. Harm/Damage
   
   CASE-SPECIFIC ALLEGATIONS:
   - Villa Via fund extraction
   - Stock adjustment fraud (R5.4M SLG)
   - Unallocated expenses manipulation
   - Revenue diversion schemes
   - Undisclosed trustee status
   
   LEGAL PRINCIPLES:
   - Common law fraud
   - Fraudulent misrepresentation
   - Constructive fraud
   - Forensic accounting standards

rules.name: resolve-breach-claim
rules.description:
Resolve breach claims (contract, fiduciary duty, statutory)
   
   BREACH TYPES:
   1. Breach of contract
   2. Breach of fiduciary duty
   3. Breach of statutory duty
   4. Breach of professional duty
   
   CASE-SPECIFIC BREACHES:
   - Fiduciary duty breaches by trustees
   - Professional duty breaches by accountants
   - Director duty breaches
   - Trust deed violations
   
   LEGAL PRINCIPLES:
   - Fiduciary duty principles
   - Professional standards
   - Director duties (Companies Act)
   - Trust law principles

rules.name: resolve-manufactured-crisis-claim
rules.description: Resolve manufactured crisis claim with sequence analysis

rules.name: resolve-unjust-enrichment-claim
rules.description:
Resolve unjust enrichment claims with enrichment analysis
   
   UNJUST ENRICHMENT ELEMENTS:
   1. Enrichment of defendant
   2. Impoverishment of plaintiff
   3. Causal connection
   4. Absence of legal justification
   5. Enrichment is unjust
   
   CASE-SPECIFIC CLAIMS:
   - Platform exploitation (RegimA Zone Ltd investment)
   - Revenue hijacking
   - Director loan system exploitation
   - Trust asset appropriation
   
   LEGAL PRINCIPLES:
   - Condictio sine causa
   - Restitution
   - Quantum meruit
   - Proprietary remedies

rules.name: resolve-conflict-of-interest-claim
rules.description:
Resolve conflict of interest claims with role analysis
   
   CONFLICT OF INTEREST ELEMENTS:
   1. Multiple incompatible roles
   2. Duty to multiple parties with conflicting interests
   3. Potential or actual compromise of duties
   4. Lack of disclosure
   
   CASE-SPECIFIC CONFLICTS:
   - Rynette: Accountant + Trustee + Creditor representative
   - Bantjies: Accountant + Undisclosed trustee
   - Peter: Founder + Trustee + Director
   
   LEGAL PRINCIPLES:
   - Fiduciary duty
   - Professional ethics
   - Disclosure requirements
   - Conflict rules

rules.name: resolve-coercion-claim
rules.description:
Resolve coercion claims with power dynamics analysis
   
   COERCION ELEMENTS:
   1. Threat or pressure
   2. Illegitimate means
   3. Overcoming of will
   4. Resulting action or inaction
   
   CASE-SPECIFIC ALLEGATIONS:
   - Medical testing weaponization
   - Trust power abuse
   - Financial pressure tactics
   - Litigation threats
   
   LEGAL PRINCIPLES:
   - Duress
   - Undue influence
   - Abuse of power
   - Procedural fairness

rules.name: analyze-temporal-sequence
rules.description:
Analyze temporal sequence of events for patterns
   
   KEY TEMPORAL PATTERNS IN CASE:
   1. Fraud discovery (June 2025) → Immediate retaliation
   2. Report submission → Card cancellations (7 Jun 2025)
   3. Coordinated sabotage sequence (Mar-Sep 2025)
   4. Manufactured urgency (sudden crisis after decades)
   
   TEMPORAL INDICATORS:
   - Immediate retaliation (< 24 hours)
   - Coordinated timing (multiple actors, synchronized)
   - Suspicious proximity (action → response correlation)
   - Pattern consistency (repeated behavior)

rules.name: detect-retaliation-patterns
rules.description: Detect retaliation patterns in timeline events with confidence scoring

rules.name: calculate-temporal-proximity
rules.description: Calculate temporal proximity between two events and return confidence score

rules.name: map-evidence-to-legal-principle
rules.description:
Map evidence to applicable legal principles
   
   EVIDENCE TYPES:
   1. Documentary evidence (bank statements, emails, contracts)
   2. Forensic evidence (accounting analysis, system logs)
   3. Timeline evidence (temporal patterns)
   4. Comparative evidence (hypocrisy, double standards)
   5. Expert evidence (technical, financial, legal)
   
   MAPPING CRITERIA:
   - Relevance to legal principle
   - Probative value
   - Admissibility
   - Weight

rules.name: calculate-evidence-strength
rules.description: Calculate evidence strength with multiple factors

rules.name: generate-burden-of-proof-analysis
rules.description: Generate burden of proof analysis for claim

rules.name: analyze-director-loan-system
rules.description:
Analyze director loan account system for hypocrisy and bad faith
   
   DIRECTOR LOAN SYSTEM ANALYSIS:
   1. Historical practice (decades)
   2. All directors participated (Peter, Jacqueline, Daniel)
   3. Proper accounting (Sage system)
   4. No board resolutions required (established practice)
   5. Peter's own withdrawals (R1.365M+ sample)
   6. Sudden characterization change (Daniel's R500K as 'gift')
   
   LEGAL IMPLICATIONS:
   - Estoppel (Peter cannot now claim practice is unauthorized)
   - Bad faith (selective application of standards)
   - Abuse of process (weaponizing normal practice)
   - Hypocrisy (double standard)

rules.name: analyze-revenue-hijacking-pattern
rules.description:
Analyze revenue hijacking and sabotage pattern
   
   REVENUE HIJACKING TIMELINE:
   - 1 Mar 2025: RegimA SA diversion started
   - 14 Apr 2025: Rynette bank letter (RWD diversion)
   - 23 May 2025: Shopify orders removed
   - 7 Jun 2025: Secret card cancellations
   - 20 Jun 2025: Email redirection (Gee instructed)
   - 11 Sep 2025: Accounts emptied
   
   PATTERN CHARACTERISTICS:
   1. Coordinated timing
   2. Multiple attack vectors
   3. Systematic approach
   4. Escalating severity
   5. Correlation with fraud discovery
   
   LEGAL IMPLICATIONS:
   - Sabotage
   - Conspiracy
   - Economic interference
   - Abuse of power

rules.name: analyze-trust-weaponization
rules.description:
Analyze weaponization of trust structure against beneficiaries
   
   TRUST WEAPONIZATION ELEMENTS:
   1. Unusual trustee powers (no beneficiary protections)
   2. Trust ownership of companies (RWD, Villa Via)
   3. Trustee control over beneficiary livelihoods
   4. Use of trust assets to attack beneficiaries
   5. Medical testing weaponization
   6. Curatorship threat
   
   LEGAL IMPLICATIONS:
   - Breach of fiduciary duty
   - Abuse of trust
   - Beneficiary oppression
   - Trust law violations

rules.name: analyze-manufactured-urgency
rules.description:
Analyze manufactured urgency claims
   
   MANUFACTURED URGENCY INDICATORS:
   1. Decades of practice → Sudden crisis
   2. No urgency until fraud discovered
   3. Artificial deadlines
   4. Exaggerated claims
   5. Timing correlation with defensive actions
   
   LEGAL IMPLICATIONS:
   - Bad faith
   - Abuse of process
   - Manufactured crisis
   - Ulterior motive

rules.name: resolve-multi-role-conflicts
rules.description:
Resolve conflicts arising from multiple incompatible roles
   
   MULTI-ROLE CONFLICTS:
   - Rynette: Accountant + Trustee + Creditor representative (TRIPLE CONFLICT)
   - Bantjies: Accountant + Undisclosed trustee
   - Peter: Founder + Trustee + Director
   
   RESOLUTION PRINCIPLES:
   - Disclosure requirement
   - Conflict management
   - Recusal where necessary
   - Independent oversight

rules.name: resolve-fiduciary-conflicts
rules.description:
Resolve fiduciary duty conflicts
   
   FIDUCIARY CONFLICTS:
   - Trustee vs. beneficiary interests
   - Director vs. shareholder interests
   - Professional vs. personal interests
   
   RESOLUTION PRINCIPLES:
   - Fiduciary primacy
   - Beneficiary protection
   - Conflict avoidance
   - Remedies for breach

rules.name: resolve-professional-duty-conflicts
rules.description:
Resolve professional duty conflicts
   
   PROFESSIONAL CONFLICTS:
   - Accountant serving as trustee of client's trust
   - Accountant serving as creditor representative
   - Undisclosed conflicts
   
   RESOLUTION PRINCIPLES:
   - Professional independence
   - Ethical standards
   - Disclosure requirements
   - Disciplinary consequences

inferenceRules.description: Agreements must be kept - the foundational principle of contract law
inferenceRules.metadata: {"provenance":"Roman law, universally recognized","applicationContext":"Binding force of contracts between parties","relatedPrinciples":["consensus-ad-idem","bona-fides"]}

inferenceRules.description: Meeting of the minds - parties must have mutual agreement
Exception of non-performance - a party need not perform if the other has not
Valid contracts require consideration or quid pro quo
No one can transfer more rights than they have
No one gives what they do not have
A thing belonging to no one - can be acquired by occupation
Hear the other side - fundamental principle of natural justice
inferenceRules.metadata: {"provenance":"Roman law, natural justice","applicationContext":"Fair hearing rights in all proceedings","relatedPrinciples":["procedural-fairness","nemo-iudex-in-causa-sua"]}

inferenceRules.description: No one should be a judge in their own cause
The burden of proof lies on the one who affirms, not on the one who denies
Specific law overrides general law
Later law overrides earlier law
Express mention of one thing excludes others
No crime without law - acts cannot be criminal unless prohibited by law
inferenceRules.metadata: {"provenance":"Enlightenment legal philosophy, international human rights","applicationContext":"Principle of legality in criminal law","relatedPrinciples":["nulla-poena-sine-lege","rule-of-law","legality"]}

inferenceRules.description: No punishment without law - punishment must be prescribed by law
inferenceRules.metadata: {"provenance":"Enlightenment legal philosophy, international human rights","applicationContext":"Sentencing and punishment legitimacy","relatedPrinciples":["nullum-crimen-sine-lege","rule-of-law"]}

inferenceRules.description: When in doubt, for the accused - presumption of innocence
inferenceRules.metadata: {"provenance":"Roman law, international human rights","applicationContext":"Criminal trials and burden of proof","relatedPrinciples":["onus-probandi","presumption-of-innocence"]}

inferenceRules.description: An act does not make one guilty unless the mind is guilty - requires both actus reus and mens rea
Loss wrongfully caused - basis of delictual liability
No injury is done to one who consents
Fault or negligence - required element for liability
The thing speaks for itself - doctrine of negligence
The constitution is supreme and all law must conform to it
Everyone is subject to the law and equal before it
Division of government into legislative, executive, and judicial branches
Humanity towards others - African philosophy of interconnectedness
inferenceRules.metadata: {"provenance":"African customary law, South African Constitution","applicationContext":"Interpretation of rights and remedies","relatedPrinciples":["human-dignity","community","solidarity"]}

inferenceRules.description: Administrative action must be authorized by law
Administrative decisions must be rational and reasonable
inferenceRules.metadata: {"provenance":"Administrative law, judicial review","applicationContext":"Substantive review of administrative action","relatedPrinciples":["reasonableness","proportionality"]}

inferenceRules.description: Fair procedures must be followed in administrative action
inferenceRules.metadata: {"provenance":"Natural justice, administrative law","applicationContext":"Procedural requirements for administrative action","relatedPrinciples":["audi-alteram-partem","nemo-iudex-in-causa-sua"]}

inferenceRules.description: Reasonable expectations created by authorities should be protected
Equity provides remedies where common law fails
One seeking equitable relief must act fairly
Equity treats all parties equally
Equitable principles supplement but do not contradict law
Burden of proof - party asserting must prove
Original evidence is preferred over secondary evidence
Evidence must be relevant to the matter at issue
Hearsay evidence is generally inadmissible
Words in statutes should be given their ordinary meaning
Literal interpretation unless it leads to absurdity
Interpret to suppress the mischief and advance the remedy
Interpret statutes according to their purpose and spirit
Time governs the act - law in force at the time governs
Rights can be lost through passage of time and inaction
Unreasonable delay can bar equitable relief
Restoration to the original position
Actual performance of contractual obligation may be ordered
Court order to do or refrain from doing something
Where there is a right, there is a remedy
Incapable of crime - children under certain age presumed incapable
Of sound mind - legal capacity requires mental competence
Good faith - acting honestly and fairly
Against good morals - unlawful conduct violating community values
From a dishonourable cause, no action arises
Utmost good faith - highest standard of honesty
He who acts through another acts himself - vicarious liability
Let the master answer - employer liability for employee acts
Cause without which not - but-for test of causation
New intervening act - breaks chain of causation
Measures must be proportionate to their objectives
inferenceRules.metadata: {"provenance":"European law, constitutional law","applicationContext":"Limitation of rights and administrative action","relatedPrinciples":["rationality","reasonableness"]}

inferenceRules.description: Decisions should be made at the most local level possible
inferenceRules.metadata: {"provenance":"European Union law, federalism","applicationContext":"Allocation of powers in multi-level governance","relatedPrinciples":["federalism","decentralization"]}

inferenceRules.description: Prohibition on returning refugees to danger
Peremptory norms of international law from which no derogation is permitted
inferenceRules.metadata: {"provenance":"Vienna Convention on the Law of Treaties","applicationContext":"Hierarchy of international legal norms","relatedPrinciples":["supremacy","hierarchy-of-norms"]}

inferenceRules.description: Treaties neither harm nor benefit third parties

jurisdictionRules.description: Create a new agent with specified identity
Detect conflicts arising from incompatible roles held by single agent
Test if two roles create inherent conflict of interest
Quantify severity of conflict on 0-1 scale based on multiple factors
Identify patterns in event timing suggesting coordination or bad faith
Select optimal combination of legal frameworks for case resolution
Map evidence to legal issues for optimal case presentation
Map evidence supporting bad faith allegations
Map evidence supporting unjust enrichment claims
Generate all pairs of roles for conflict detection
Create a conflict report for incompatible role pair
Create a temporal pattern record
Create a framework selection record
Create an evidence mapping record
Resolve sabotage claim with enhanced evidence mapping

Resolve bad faith claims with contextual analysis
   
   BAD FAITH ELEMENTS:
   1. Dishonest or improper motive
   2. Abuse of process or power
   3. Lack of genuine belief in claim
   4. Ulterior purpose
   
   CASE-SPECIFIC INDICATORS:
   - Director loan system hypocrisy (Peter's own withdrawals vs. characterization of Daniel's)
   - Timing of interdict (immediately after fraud discovery)
   - Manufactured urgency claims
   - Selective application of standards
   - Weaponization of trust powers
   
   LEGAL PRINCIPLES:
   - Bona fides requirement
   - Abuse of process
   - Estoppel
   - Clean hands doctrine

Resolve fraud claims with forensic analysis
   
   FRAUD ELEMENTS:
   1. Misrepresentation (false statement)
   2. Knowledge of falsity (scienter)
   3. Intention to deceive
   4. Reliance
   5. Harm/Damage
   
   CASE-SPECIFIC ALLEGATIONS:
   - Villa Via fund extraction
   - Stock adjustment fraud (R5.4M SLG)
   - Unallocated expenses manipulation
   - Revenue diversion schemes
   - Undisclosed trustee status
   
   LEGAL PRINCIPLES:
   - Common law fraud
   - Fraudulent misrepresentation
   - Constructive fraud
   - Forensic accounting standards

Resolve breach claims (contract, fiduciary duty, statutory)
   
   BREACH TYPES:
   1. Breach of contract
   2. Breach of fiduciary duty
   3. Breach of statutory duty
   4. Breach of professional duty
   
   CASE-SPECIFIC BREACHES:
   - Fiduciary duty breaches by trustees
   - Professional duty breaches by accountants
   - Director duty breaches
   - Trust deed violations
   
   LEGAL PRINCIPLES:
   - Fiduciary duty principles
   - Professional standards
   - Director duties (Companies Act)
   - Trust law principles
Resolve manufactured crisis claim with sequence analysis

Resolve unjust enrichment claims with enrichment analysis
   
   UNJUST ENRICHMENT ELEMENTS:
   1. Enrichment of defendant
   2. Impoverishment of plaintiff
   3. Causal connection
   4. Absence of legal justification
   5. Enrichment is unjust
   
   CASE-SPECIFIC CLAIMS:
   - Platform exploitation (RegimA Zone Ltd investment)
   - Revenue hijacking
   - Director loan system exploitation
   - Trust asset appropriation
   
   LEGAL PRINCIPLES:
   - Condictio sine causa
   - Restitution
   - Quantum meruit
   - Proprietary remedies

Resolve conflict of interest claims with role analysis
   
   CONFLICT OF INTEREST ELEMENTS:
   1. Multiple incompatible roles
   2. Duty to multiple parties with conflicting interests
   3. Potential or actual compromise of duties
   4. Lack of disclosure
   
   CASE-SPECIFIC CONFLICTS:
   - Rynette: Accountant + Trustee + Creditor representative
   - Bantjies: Accountant + Undisclosed trustee
   - Peter: Founder + Trustee + Director
   
   LEGAL PRINCIPLES:
   - Fiduciary duty
   - Professional ethics
   - Disclosure requirements
   - Conflict rules

Resolve coercion claims with power dynamics analysis
   
   COERCION ELEMENTS:
   1. Threat or pressure
   2. Illegitimate means
   3. Overcoming of will
   4. Resulting action or inaction
   
   CASE-SPECIFIC ALLEGATIONS:
   - Medical testing weaponization
   - Trust power abuse
   - Financial pressure tactics
   - Litigation threats
   
   LEGAL PRINCIPLES:
   - Duress
   - Undue influence
   - Abuse of power
   - Procedural fairness

Analyze temporal sequence of events for patterns
   
   KEY TEMPORAL PATTERNS IN CASE:
   1. Fraud discovery (June 2025) → Immediate retaliation
   2. Report submission → Card cancellations (7 Jun 2025)
   3. Coordinated sabotage sequence (Mar-Sep 2025)
   4. Manufactured urgency (sudden crisis after decades)
   
   TEMPORAL INDICATORS:
   - Immediate retaliation (< 24 hours)
